title: macOS keybindings: audit and fix Cmd/Option/Fn navigation and deletion
task:
macOS users expect Command/Option/Fn keybindings that the norn-tui input field's mapper claims to support but that may not be firing end-to-end. The InputAction mappings already exist in crates/norn-tui/src/input/keybindings.rs (SUPER for Cmd, ALT for Option, Home/End fallbacks). The Kitty PushFlags init in crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/setup.rs enables SUPER + REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS delivery on Kitty-protocol terminals. Despite this, the bindings don't all work in practice. Audit the key-event delivery chain, identify what's actually broken, and fix it. Do not assume 'mapper exists therefore wired' — verify each binding end-to-end.

OUTCOME — the user should experience standard macOS behaviour in the input field:
- Cmd+Left → cursor to start of current logical line
- Cmd+Right → cursor to end of current logical line
- Cmd+Up → cursor to start of buffer (top of input)
- Cmd+Down → cursor to end of buffer (bottom of input)
- Cmd+Backspace → delete from cursor to start of current line
- Cmd+Delete (Fn+Cmd+Backspace on a Mac laptop, where Fn reverses direction) → delete from cursor to end of current line
- Option+Left/Right → move cursor word-by-word
- Option+Backspace → delete word back
- Option+Delete (Fn+Option+Backspace) → delete word forward
- The Fn-reversal works because the OS already translates Fn+Backspace into a Forward Delete key code at the keyboard driver layer — the terminal sees KeyCode::Delete, not a separate Fn modifier. The mapper does not need to handle Fn; it needs to handle Backspace and Delete as different key codes (which the existing code already does, but verify the delivery).
- On terminals that do not advertise the Kitty protocol (so SUPER is not delivered), provide sensible fallbacks: Home/End already exist for line-start/end. Add Ctrl+A / Ctrl+E readline-style fallbacks for the same, Ctrl+U for delete-to-line-start, and document the limitation that buffer-start/end (Cmd+Up/Down) has no fallback without Kitty.

requirements.description: Audit the key-delivery chain from terminal to InputAction. Add a `tracing::debug!` log line in `map_key_event` in crates/norn-tui/src/input/keybindings.rs that emits the raw KeyCode + Modifiers received and the InputAction returned (if any). This is diagnostic instrumentation — KEEP it gated behind tracing's debug level (compiled in, runtime-controllable via RUST_LOG). The agent running this task can then read the log output of a real session to see which modifiers actually arrive and decide what to fix. Do not enable this at info level (would be noisy in production).
requirements.acceptance: A debug log line is emitted per call with structured fields (key, mods, action). cargo test -p norn-tui passes (the existing mapper tests should still pass; the debug log is non-functional). cargo clippy clean.

requirements.description: Verify each macOS-conventional binding fires the expected InputAction. The mapper tests in keybindings.rs cover most of these as synthetic KeyEvent → InputAction round-trips. Audit the test list, fill any gaps, and ensure: SUPER+Left→LineStart, SUPER+Right→LineEnd, SUPER+Up→BufferStart, SUPER+Down→BufferEnd, SUPER+Backspace→DeleteToLineStart, SUPER+Delete→DeleteToLineEnd, ALT+Left→WordLeft, ALT+Right→WordRight, ALT+Backspace→DeleteWordBack, ALT+Delete→DeleteWordForward, Backspace→Backspace, Delete→Delete, Home→LineStart, End→LineEnd. If a test is missing for any of these, add it.
requirements.acceptance: Every binding above has a unit test in crates/norn-tui/src/input/keybindings.rs that synthesises a KeyEvent and asserts the InputAction. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Add readline-style Ctrl-based fallbacks for line navigation and deletion. On terminals without the Kitty protocol, Command is not delivered, so the SUPER-based bindings never fire. Add: Ctrl+A → LineStart (in addition to existing Home), Ctrl+E → LineEnd (in addition to existing End), Ctrl+U → DeleteToLineStart. These are standard readline / emacs bindings that work across every terminal. Do NOT add a fallback for BufferStart / BufferEnd — those are Cmd+Up/Down specifically, and Ctrl+Up/Ctrl+Down can mean other things in other contexts; document the limitation in the InputAction rustdoc instead.
requirements.acceptance: Three new unit tests covering Ctrl+A, Ctrl+E, Ctrl+U mapping to their respective actions. Existing Ctrl+C, Ctrl+O, Ctrl+E, Ctrl+K tests still pass (note: Ctrl+E is already taken for ToggleThinking — pick a different fallback for LineEnd, e.g. keep End as the only LineEnd fallback OR remap ToggleThinking to a different key. If the latter, surface the trade-off in the task report — do not silently change a user-facing binding without flagging it). Suggested resolution if Ctrl+E is taken: leave LineEnd's only fallback as the End key, document that Cmd+Right needs Kitty and End is the universal alternative.

requirements.description: Verify the SUPER bit is actually arriving from the terminal. Add an end-to-end smoke test (an `#[test]` that constructs a KeyEvent with Modifiers::SUPER | Modifiers::ALT, runs it through the mapper, and asserts SUPER wins per the documented precedence — Command beats Option). Also add a test where Modifiers::SUPER is absent (simulating a non-Kitty terminal) and verify the fallback path (Home/End/Ctrl+A/Ctrl+E) is reachable. These tests should fail loudly if the precedence logic in map_key_event ever regresses.
requirements.acceptance: Two tests: super_takes_precedence_over_alt_on_left (may already exist — verify) and non_kitty_fallback_reaches_line_start_via_home. cargo test passes.

requirements.description: Document the Fn-key behaviour. Add a rustdoc paragraph at the top of keybindings.rs explaining that the Fn key on Mac laptops is handled at the OS keyboard-driver layer and translates Fn+Backspace → Forward Delete key code (KeyCode::Delete) before reaching the terminal. The mapper therefore does not need to handle a Fn modifier explicitly; it just needs to handle KeyCode::Backspace and KeyCode::Delete as different actions, which it already does. This documentation is for future maintainers (and dispels the apparent gap).
requirements.acceptance: Module-level rustdoc updated. cargo build -p norn-tui --tests does not introduce missing-docs warnings.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/keybindings.rs — the mapper. Existing mappings for every binding above are already present (verified in current code). The tests already cover most of them.
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/setup.rs — KITTY_FLAGS at line 27 already includes DISAMBIGUATE_ESCAPE_CODES | REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS, which is the right combo for SUPER delivery. Kitty PushFlags is conditionally pushed at line 91 only when caps.kitty_keyboard is true.
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/caps.rs — caps.kitty_keyboard is set by terminal detection.
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs — input handler that calls map_key_event with caps and popup_open.
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/navigation.rs — implementations of word_left, word_right, line_start, line_end, buffer_start, buffer_end, delete_word_back, delete_word_forward, delete_to_line_start, delete_to_line_end. All exist and have unit tests.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — DO NOT REIMPLEMENT:
- The mapper logic (SUPER>ALT>bare precedence)
- The navigation/deletion editor methods
- Kitty PushFlags init
- Most of the existing tests

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE (Tom's terminal behaviour suggests):
- Either the user's terminal isn't actually advertising the Kitty protocol despite Tom's belief (verify caps.kitty_keyboard detection logic), OR
- The SUPER bit is being swallowed somewhere downstream of map_key_event, OR
- The bindings work but the user's terminal eats Cmd+arrow at the OS level (e.g. Terminal.app's window-shortcut behaviour).

The diagnostic log (R1) is the cheap way to find out which it is. Read the log output, then act.

STANDARDS — per CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo in production code; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; thiserror in library code; tests cover happy path AND failure path.

VERIFICATION: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui.
